rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (bot rate-limit notice + bare 'lgtm'), so both candidates trivially achieve full recall on a genuinely trivial rename PR. Both did solid diligence (grep verification, upstream cross-check) with plausible but partly unverifiable claims about upstream line numbers, so precision is comparably high. Y edges ahead on actionability by surfacing one concrete, diff-grounded observation (inconsistent naming convention vs. other openai_serving_* attributes) with a specific file:line and suggested fix, whereas X's only non-blocking note argues nothing should change, offering no actionable output.
